summary: Decompiled two small functions: `IsNameUnique` in `src/melee/mn/mnname.c` and `un_80305D00` in `src/melee/ty/toy.c`, and updated the `IsNameUnique` declaration in `mnname.h` from unknown return/params to `bool IsNameUnique(s32 slot)`. The automated report says both new matches reached 100.00%, adding 316 matched bytes total. The PR body appears unrelated to the actual title/diff, so the diff and report are stronger evidence than the body summary.

key_files.role: Replaced `/// #IsNameUnique` stub marker with a C implementation scanning up to 0x78 name slots, checking valid entries, retrieving persistent name data, and comparing name strings.
Updated the public prototype for `IsNameUnique` from `UNK_RET IsNameUnique(UNK_PARAMS)` to `bool IsNameUnique(s32 slot)`.

Replaced `/// #un_80305D00` stub marker with a C implementation that scans four controller pad statuses and returns the first normal stick X value whose absolute value exceeds 0.1, or the last checked value.

what_changed: `IsNameUnique(s32 slot)` was implemented as a `bool` function using a bounded `for (i = 0; i < 0x78; i++)` loop.

`IsNameUnique` calls `IsNameValid(i & 0xFF)`, conditionally reads `GetPersistentNameData((u8) i)->namedata`, breaks on NULL name data, and returns true when `CompareNameStrings(namedata, slot) == 0`; otherwise it returns false.
`mnname.h` was synchronized with the recovered `IsNameUnique` signature.
`un_80305D00(void)` was implemented as a `float` function using a four-iteration loop over `HSD_PadCopyStatus[(u8) i].nml_stickX`.
`un_80305D00` manually computes the absolute value of the stick X value for threshold testing while preserving `ret` as the original signed value to return.
The automated GALE01 report records two new 100.00% matches: `un_80305D00` and `IsNameUnique`.
smart_moves: The `IsNameUnique` header declaration was updated alongside the body, avoiding a stale `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` prototype after the signature was recovered.
Both functions use compact natural loops rather than duplicated generated-looking checks.

`un_80305D00` keeps the original signed stick value in `ret` while using an absolute-value temporary for the deadzone threshold comparison, which reflects a common matching need where the tested value and returned value differ.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function body proves a signature, update the owning header in the same change.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `mnname.h` changes `UNK_RET IsNameUnique(UNK_PARAMS)` to `bool IsNameUnique(s32 slot)`

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small repeated input/name scans can often be expressed as natural bounded `for` loops, even when casts or temporaries are needed for matching.
For input deadzone logic, preserve the signed value for return separately from the absolute-value temporary used for threshold checks when the assembly requires both roles.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `un_80305D00` assigns `ret = val`, negates `val` only for comparison, and returns `ret`

naming_conventions.name: IsNameUnique
naming_conventions.convention: Semantic function name retained and signature clarified as returning `bool` with parameter `s32 slot`.

naming_conventions.name: slot
naming_conventions.convention: Recovered parameter name reflects the slot passed to `CompareNameStrings`.

naming_conventions.name: un_80305D00
naming_conventions.convention: Address-style name retained because the PR evidence does not provide a semantic name.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use explicit casts on loop index accesses where needed: `i & 0xFF`, `(u8) i`, and `HSD_PadCopyStatus[(u8) i]`.
Use a temporary `ret` to preserve the original signed floating-point value while modifying another temporary for absolute-value comparison.
Use a ternary assignment to select NULL versus field access in `IsNameUnique` before the break-on-NULL check.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Synchronize recovered function signatures with owning headers
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2323 implemented `bool IsNameUnique(s32 slot)` in `mnname.c` and updated `mnname.h` from `UNK_RET IsNameUnique(UNK_PARAMS)` to the recovered signature in the same change.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Preserve signed return value separately from absolute-value threshold temporary
accepted_candidate_records.text: In `un_80305D00`, the source stores `ret = val`, conditionally negates `val` only for comparison with `0.1F`, and returns `ret`, showing a narrow matching pattern for input deadzone scans where the compared magnitude and returned signed value differ.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body descriptions of `grIceMt_801F91EC` or `grKongo_801D8078`; they conflict with the PR title, changed files, diff, and match report.
Do not infer a semantic name for `un_80305D00`; the PR retained the address-style name and provides no naming rationale.
